title: Avoid Assignments within Sub-expressions
description: Assigning a value to a variable inside a larger expression, such as within a loop or a conditional statement, can make the code less readable and potentially lead to errors. This rule ignores assignments in conditions of while statements and assignments enclosed in relational expressions and also ignores chained assignments, including compound assignments.
recommendation: Instead of making assignments within sub-expressions, consider breaking down the expression and making the assignment separately to increase the readability of the code.
impact: Improving this aspect enhances the readability of the code, which reduces the likelihood of errors and increases maintainability.

checks.message: Instead of making assignments within sub-expressions, consider breaking down the expression and making the assignment separately to increase the readability of the code.
